text:
17-year-old Algerian gymnast Kaylia Nemour soared to a gold medal finish during Sunday芒聙聶s uneven bars final after an absolutely captivating individual performance.脗

Nemour scored a whopping 15.7 to win her first ever gold medal and become the first Olympic medalist in gymnastics from Africa. She defeated reigning world champion, China芒聙聶s Qiu Qiyuan (15.5), who took home silver, and American gymnast Sunisa Lee, who took home bronze.

Among those watching at Bercy Arena was Simone Biles, who was no doubt in the house to cheer on her compatriot, Lee.

But even Biles couldn芒聙聶t contain her excitement after seeing Nemour flawlessly stick the landing and raise both her arms in the air. Nemour immediately teared up on the mat, knowing she delivered possibly the best routine of her young Olympic career.

Biles was seen applauding Nemour's performance with a wide smile on her face. Nemour and Lee also shared a sweet embrace after Nemour芒聙聶s score was announced.脗

Algeria芒聙聶s Kaylia Nemour put on one of her best performances in the uneven bars final to win gold, and Simone Biles loved it. 冒聼楼聡#ParisOlympicspic.twitter.com/iXDorLBrRW

The Algerian breakout star finished fifth in the women芒聙聶s all-around final earlier this week but convincingly earned her spot on the podium on Sunday with a stunning show that left fans breathless.

People were especially happy for Nemour to win while representing Algeria, instead of France. Nemour holds dual citizenship in both countries but chose to compete for Algeria this summer due to areporteddispute between the French gymnastics federation and her club, Avoine Beaumont.

OH MY. 冒聼聵虏What a stellar uneven bars routine from Algeria芒聙聶s Kaylia Nemour!#ParisOlympics冒聼聯潞 NBC and Peacockpic.twitter.com/gZPZkZ0MFd

france did everything to block kaylia from switching to algeria and qualifying to the olympics 芒聙娄 SHE JUST WON THE OLYMPIC GOLD MEDAL SAY HER NAME !!!!!pic.twitter.com/PMHFPaByZO

15.700 POUR KAYLIA NEMOUR. OH KAYLIA TU ES MAGNIFIQUE 冒聼聵颅芒聺陇茂赂聫冒聼聡漏冒聼聡驴pic.twitter.com/oKYZuftFFX

kaylia nemour is gonna get a gold medal for algeria in her home country of france after everything that芒聙聶s happened with the federation芒聙娄. absolutely poetic

Kaylia Nemour winning ub gold IN france on the 20th anniversary of France芒聙聶s one and only wag olympic medal after they continually screwed her over, this is the best day of my life!!!pic.twitter.com/niZkniwaov

No one swings like Kaylia Nemour. No one.

Kristen Wong is a staff writer on the Breaking and Trending News team at Sports Illustrated. She has been a sports journalist since 2020. Before joining SI in November 2023, Wong covered four NFL teams as an associate editor with the FanSided NFL Network and worked as a staff writer for the brand芒聙聶s flagship site. Outside of work, she has dreams of running her own sporty dive bar.

description: The Algerian gymnast shocked the world on Sunday.
